criteria.description: Only applies to non proposed fucntions, the proposed by the exam functions are evaluates elsewhere. Functions are correctly implemented according to their headers: the logic respects the specification, the return type is appropriate, and the parameters are correctly used inside the body.

rules.description: The implementation does not follow the specification indicated by the header (e.g., logic incompatible with the description).
The actual return type is inadequate compared to the type declared in the header.
Parameters are unused or incorrectly used in the function body.

criteria.description: Only applies to functions called from the main function. Functions are correctly called: parameters are passed correctly, and the return value is used or stored when appropriate. Ignore small mistakes ore type missmatch when in C the result is not too different.

rules.description: The function call uses an incorrect number, order, or type of arguments compared to the header.
Arguments are incorrectly assigned (e.g., type mismatch or inappropriate passing semantics).
The return value is not stored or used when it should be.
The defined function is not called anywhere in the code.

criteria.description: Functions are declared or defined in a correct order that allows calling them without linking or compilation errors.

rules.description: A function is called before its declaration or prototype (and no valid prototype exists beforehand).
A required prototype is missing to support the order of calls used.
Duplicated or inconsistent declarations that prevent a correct order of use.
